changes: OpenClaw shipped stable v2026.4.24 on 2026-04-25 18:15 UTC, keeping the category anchor on a near-daily release cadence.
SwarmClaw shipped stable v1.6.0 on 2026-04-25 23:47 UTC, extending its fast control-plane/orchestration release cadence.
Hermes Agent did not post a newer release than v2026.4.23, but it climbed to roughly 117.1k stars in the 2026-04-26 scan.
PicoClaw posted another nightly build on 2026-04-26 01:06 UTC, while ZeroClaw materially rewrote its README positioning toward ownership, installability, and explicit runtime controls.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw is still visibly acting like the pace-setter, which keeps the reference implementation strategically central.
SwarmClaw reaching v1.6.0 matters more than routine star drift because it reinforces that the orchestration layer is becoming a real product lane, not just an experiment.

Hermes' continued acceleration strengthens the learning-loop narrative, while ZeroClaw's messaging shift suggests lightweight competitors may increasingly borrow security and sovereignty language as they mature.
